1.title: The shortest book
1.body: By word count in the original Greek, Third John is the shortest book in the Bible. It is a private letter addressed to a single friend, Gaius.

1.template: I have no greater {0} than to hear that my children walk in truth
they went forth, taking nothing of the {0}

1.title: Diotrephes the contentious
1.body: Diotrephes is named only here in scripture—a local leader who “loveth to have the preeminence,” refused to receive John's messengers, and even cast out those who would.

1.question: What does John say Diotrephes loves (v. 9)?
options: To have the preeminence among them

1.title: Three men named in the letter

pairs.right: the beloved, who walks in truth
hath good report of all men
